# Intro to Rust for R Developers {.unnumbered}

We've got 3 hours to cover a lot of ground! My goal is to get you all to a place where you can begin to *write vectorized code* by the end of the day.

I've prepared more content than I think we can actually cover—let's prove me wrong!

::: aside

I'm going to skip a bunch of content that would be considered "foundational" Rust like `HashMap`s and traits. My goal is to get you to write usable code without having to feel like you're getting a CS degree.

:::

## Timeline

This is the conservative timeline for the workshop. So long as we make it to "Mapping over iterators" we will be in good shape for the next workshop!


|Section                |Duration | End time |
|:----------------------|:--------|:--------|
|Intro & Why Rust       |0:10     |9:10 AM  |
|Hello, world!          |0:10     |9:20 AM  |
|Primitive Types        |0:10     |9:30 AM  |
|Control Flow           |0:10     |9:40 AM  |
|Arrays & Vectors       |0:10     |9:50 AM  |
|for loops              |0:10     |10:00 AM |
|Break                  |0:05     |10:05 AM |
|Mutability             |0:05     |10:10 AM |
|Mutable Vectors        |0:10     |10:20 AM |
|Functions              |0:10     |10:30 AM |
|Ownership              |0:10     |10:40 AM |
|Iterators              |0:15     |10:55 AM |
|Mapping over Iterators |0:10     |11:05 AM |
|Break                  |0:05     |11:10 AM |
|Defining Structs       |0:10     |11:20 AM |
|Struct Methods         |0:10     |11:30 AM |
|Enumerations           |0:05     |11:35 AM |
|Options                |0:10     |11:45 AM |
|Result                 |0:05     |11:50 AM |
|Adding a crate: anyhow |0:10     |12:00 PM |
